message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 98726ca (branch bot/outline-agent-box). Inherited work is SOLID and PASSES: 19 tests green in ~2.8s. execute_python is un-bricked. The compute-reachability P1 is HALF-CLOSED: the SURFACING half is DONE — summary_for_llm(include_results=True) emits a computed-stats digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276. The RENDER half is STILL OPEN at outline_agent.py:1609 — a computed number cannot yet render through the verified [#calc:] lane. NOT BUILT YET: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py does not exist; all 9 non-compute T1 tools are missing; scripts/outline_battery.py does not exist; the fold-in seam (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792) is NOT yet factored as a reusable unit.

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
Fable's gate verdict was CONTINUE. Reason: Diff verified line-by-line and by independent re-runs: stats out-param is behavior-neutral (counter only, inside existing refusal branches), observability surfaces in menu/WARNING/sweep as claimed, 112/112 + 11/11 S0..S4=0 reproduced. Faithfulness lanes untouched. But the mission bar includes the full-corpus live number, which is unmeasured due to the missing key — a measurement not taken is not a sign-off, so verdict is CONTINUE with an explicit concurrence to HOLD: no faithfulness-safe code work remains worth landing before the credential arrives.
DO THIS NEXT (precise): HOLD at BLOCKED — land no further code. Each round: (1) re-check os.getenv('OPENROUTER_API_KEY'); (2) the instant it is set, run the staged command exactly: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus <346-basket cp3 dump> --min-baskets 346, and record per-basket cp4_used==agentic, the false_merge_guard_hits doi=0 title=0 line (scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py:238), and the RACE/DeepTRACE score in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md; (3) keep the credential escalation as the sole standing item. If any guard hit is non-zero on the live corpus, that (and only that) reopens code work: the key-level union-find remap flagged in outline_digest.py:306.
Gap to top: Solely the live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE measurement, hard-blocked on the absent OPENROUTER_API_KEY (re-verified independently this round: os.getenv -> False). Every offline metric is at ceiling and was re-reproduced by the reviewer: outline+digest 112/112, T2 battery 11/11 with S0..S4=0 (run 20260711T212138Z), ruling locked with test + tripwire, 0 guard hits on the real cp3 snapshot. No credential, no live number — and no proxy is acceptable.

RULES:
- Actually EXERCISE every change (run it; a heartbeat/concurrency probe, a real test, a real score —
  py_compile is NOT proof). Commit what passes.
- MEASURE the success metric ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).) with a real number this round if at all possible.
- End by writing ONE precise question for Fable in "needs_fable" — a decision or a
  correctness/faithfulness/gap-to-top judgment. Do NOT ask Fable to read or search the codebase; you
  already did that. Give Fable the diff summary + the measurement, not a reading assignment.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

with ``PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1`` + the §9.1.8 model lock) over one or many saved
cp3 corpora and ASSERTS, per corpus, the mission invariant: cp4_used ==
"agentic" NEVER fallback-plain. A seed fallback (``cp4_used == "agentic-
degraded-seed"``) is accepted ONLY when the degrade reason is a GLM-5.2
reasoning truncation (``ReasoningFirstTruncationError``,
openrouter_client.py:257) — the ONE sanctioned degrade. Any other degrade
reason, or the plain non-agentic path, is a HARD FAIL (a silent capability
downgrade the operator never asked for). It also records whether the MOAT is
ARMED for that corpus: the agentic loop must export a verified-compute
registry (``quantified_models``) AND per-section render-ready ``[#calc:]``
sentences (``calc_claims``) so a computed number will RENDER through the
verified lane (metric (a)). The sweep does NOT itself score RACE/DeepTRACE —
that is the downstream evaluator on the composed report — but it proves the

content.text: Everything offline is reconfirmed at ceiling and the live sweep is armed but hard-blocked on the absent key. I land no code per the standing HOLD order. Let me append one honest dated line to the progress log (doc only, not code).

content.content:
2026-07-11 STEP 8 (T1 corroboration_profile + decide-menu scaling + staged full-corpus sweep; SOLE GATE = CREDENTIALS): (1) LANDED corroboration_profile (redesign Category D #27, the last open T1 tool) in outline_toolkit.py — per-basket corroboration at WORK level via a union-find over strong-identity tokens (normalized DOI / source-url / title-key), STRICTER than the cp3 url-first _same_work_key so same-DOI-different-URL copies collapse to ONE work. Flags the single-work MASQUERADE (>=2 rows -> 1 distinct work) that redesign.md:217 names as a FAIL (single-work corroboration silently counted as multi-work), surfaces distinct-WORK counts NOT member counts, and raises a digest-vs-recompute disagreement tripwire (proven: cp3 digest said 3 works, honest recompute said 1). 6 unit tests + 2 battery cases (masquerade flagged; genuine 2-work NOT false-flagged). (2) LANDED decide-prompt CORE/index scaling (redesign 'Scaling past 20 tools'): ToolDefinition gains tags/core (defaults => byte-identical existing call sites); ToolRegistry.get_decide_menu is byte-identical to the full listing at/below core_threshold and collapses to CORE-in-full + a categorized one-line INDEX past it; wired at env-tunable PG_OUTLINE_DECIDE_CORE_THRESHOLD default 60 (today's 22-tool registry still prints IN FULL => ZERO live-prompt change; collapse arms automatically when the MCP layer grows the registry). New list_tools meta-tool = the on-demand discovery path. 5 new tests (byte-identical below; collapse above; list_tools by name+category). (3) STAGED the one-command full-corpus gate scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py: runs run_outline_agent_or_legacy over one/many cp3 corpora with PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the model lock and asserts per corpus cp4_used==agentic — NEVER fallback-plain, seed fallback accepted ONLY on the sanctioned GLM-5.2 ReasoningFirstTruncationError (openrouter_client.py:257); records moat-armed (quantified_models + calc_claims exported). --dry-run exercises the whole assertion surface OFFLINE (proven: 5 cp4 cases + basket counter all correct, exit 0). 7 unit tests lock the invariant. MEASUREMENT this round: outline suite 107/107 green (was 63); T2 battery 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (added h_corr1/h_corr2); ReAct/tool suites 228+5 green; sweep dry-run PASSED. SOLE REMAINING GATE (ESCALATED — hard external blocker, NOT a code issue): the live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number cannot be produced because there is NO LLM API key in the environment. VERIFIED THIS ROUND: `python3 -c "import os; print(bool(os.getenv('OPENROUTER_API_KEY')))"` => False; the ONLY credential present is CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ACCESS_TOKEN. The agentic outliner calls GLM-5.2 via OPENROUTER_API_KEY (openrouter_client.py:58); with it empty, run_outline_agent_or_legacy cannot execute a single decide turn, so metric (a)-FULL-CORPUS and the RACE/DeepTRACE sweep are BLOCKED. Everything offline is green and the sweep is staged to fire the instant OPENROUTER_API_KEY is provided: `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus <346-basket cp3 dump> --min-baskets 346`. NO proxy/fake number was produced — the benchmark bar requires the real live number and that is credential-gated. ACTION NEEDED FROM OPERATOR: drop OPENROUTER_API_KEY into the env. Commits 9182925, 8f5aecb, 9c796c3.
2026-07-11 STEP 9 (digest DOI fold + calc_claims duplicate-title guard — contained planner-quality fixes while credentials block the live number): Fable's CONTINUE ruling identified one high-value contained improvement landable while the 346-basket live sweep is credential-blocked. (1) LANDED a normalized-DOI fold in _build_alias_map (outline_digest.py) running BEFORE the item-2 title fold, mirroring it exactly (same overlap-adopt + item-4 false-merge guard) but keyed on _normalize_doi (copied byte-for-byte from finding_dedup so the module keeps its zero-dep contract). Same-DOI-different-URL copies the cp3 url-first same_work_id left SPLIT now unify into ONE work, so the -work_count basket sort (outline_digest.py:533) no longer ranks a pseudo-corroborated same-DOI basket above a genuine multi-work basket. Byte-identical when evidence=None OR no >=2 rows share a usable 10. DOI. NOTE: this is planner-quality only — verified_support_origin_count (the shipped-report DeepTRACE corroboration stat, multi_section_generator.py:126-222) and the [CITE:ev_xxx] render lane are UNTOUCHED, so zero faithfulness/DeepTRACE render liability; finding_dedup._same_work_key precedence (kill-switched STEP-4 design) also untouched. (2) CLOSED the deferred calc_claims duplicate-section-title uniqueness guard (_build_calc_claims_map, outline_agent.py): duplicate draft plans sharing one title had their ev_ids SPLIT across list entries, so auto-home overlap was measured against only ONE half — mis-homing a claim to a competitor section that overlapped each half more. Now folds duplicate titles into ONE entry with the UNION of their ev_ids (dict first-seen order => deterministic). PROVEN discriminating: OLD list logic homes the split-evidence claim to 'Costs' (overlap 2 on the competitor), NEW union homes to 'Efficacy' (overlap 2 on its full set, first-max). MEASUREMENT this round: +4 tests (item5 DOI fold unify + distinct-DOI no-fold guard; END-TO-END build_outline_digest basket_work_corroboration==1 on the 3-row same-DOI-different-URL fixture with corroboration_profile.digest_disagreement then reading False; calc_claims duplicate-title guard) — outline+digest suite 111/111 green (was 107); T2 battery 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0. SOLE REMAINING GATE unchanged: OPENROUTER_API_KEY absent (verified again this round: env has no key), so the live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number stays credential-blocked; no proxy number produced. Commit 52d5b5f.
2026-07-11 STEP 10 (LOCK THE DOI false-merge RULING + observability tripwire; credentials still the sole gate): Fable's ruling KEEP the len(stamped)>=2 refusal at _build_alias_map (outline_digest.py DOI branch) — it is the correct conservative choice and its worry-case (two MULTI-member cp3 url: groups sharing one DOI) has ZERO incidence on the real corpus. Landed two changes to lock it: (1) TEST test_item4_doi_two_multi_member_url_groups_shared_doi_stay_separate encodes exactly that case — two multi-member cp3 url: groups (url:groupA=[a1,a2], url:groupB=[b1,b2]) sharing DOI 10.9/shared plus an UNCLAIMED same-DOI row u1 — and asserts NO merge: a1,a2 keep url:groupA; b1,b2 keep url:groupB; url:groupA != url:groupB; only u1 folds to doi:10.9/shared; and the tripwire records exactly 1 guard hit. (2) OBSERVABILITY TRIPWIRE: _build_alias_map gained an optional stats out-param (byte-identical when omitted) that counts DOI/title groups hitting the false-merge REFUSAL branch; build_outline_digest surfaces the counts on OutlineDigestMenu.{doi,title}_false_merge_guard_hits and logs a WARNING when non-zero (never silent); scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py computes the count per corpus (independent of the agent build) and prints a per-corpus "false_merge_guard_hits: doi=N title=M" line with a NON-ZERO audit flag. REAL MEASUREMENT confirming the ruling on /workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json (55 same_work_groups, 385 reconstructed member rows, 13 with usable 10. DOIs parsed from member_urls): DOIs spanning >=2 cp3 groups = 0; measured guard hits = 0 (dict empty). The refusal never bites on the real corpus, so a member-level union-find remap is unjustified. Faithfulness intact: DOI fold is planner-quality only; [CITE:ev_xxx]/[#calc:] render lanes untouched. MEASUREMENT this round: outline+digest suite 112/112 green (was 111, +1 new test); T2 battery 11/11 PASS S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0; sweep --dry-run PASSED. SOLE REMAINING GATE unchanged and ESCALATED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY absent (verified again: os.getenv -> False), so the live 346-basket cp4_used=agentic RACE/DeepTRACE number stays credential-blocked; no proxy number produced. The staged sweep fires the instant the key lands: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py --corpus <346-basket cp3 dump> --min-baskets 346.
